# go-bpfman

A proof-of-concept rewrite of [bpfman](https://github.com/bpfman/bpfman)
in Go. The upstream project is written in Rust.

This project is in a very fluid state and should be treated as
experimental.

<!-- BEGIN GENERATED RUST PARITY -->
## Rust parity

<!-- Generated by `make parity-readme` from docs/parity/cases.yaml and
     the transcripts under docs/parity/outputs/. Do not edit this
     section by hand; edit cases.yaml and regenerate. -->

go-bpfman aims for behavioural parity with Rust bpfman across the shared
CLI surface: `load`, `attach`, `detach`, `unload`, `list`, `get`. This
section records reproducible evidence for that aim, comparing the two
implementations on the same BPF object files and commands. The full
evidence -- raw transcripts, the driver scripts, and the detailed
findings -- lives under [`docs/parity/`](docs/parity/).

Scope is CLI only: gRPC, the operator, and image-build flows are not
covered here. Compared against Rust bpfman `8e5a9d29` (`bpfman 0.6.0`)
and go-bpfman `c392521a`, on Linux 6.18.33.

### Coverage at a glance

| Area | State | Verdict |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CLI program lifecycle (10 program types) | proven | parity; Go ahead on kretprobe/uretprobe |
| Dispatcher ordering, slot reuse and exhaustion | proven | parity |
| Load/attach flags (metadata, application, global, proceed-on, direction, priority, netns, offset, pid) | proven | parity; global 0x and tie-break unclear |
| Error handling and idempotency | proven | parity on failure class; exit codes differ |
| `--map-owner-id` map sharing | proven | Go ahead (Rust bug) |
| `--container-pid` | proven | Go-only (Rust unsupported) |
| Image load and `--pull-policy` | proven | parity |
| Image build / inspect / sign | not tested | -- |
| gRPC daemon (`serve`) | not tested | -- |
| CSI driver | not tested | -- |
| Library / embedding API | not tested | -- |

`State` is the column to scan for remaining work: `proven` means parity
is computed and gated by `cmd/parity-compare`; `not tested` means this
evidence does not yet cover it. The not-tested surfaces exist in both
implementations -- `not tested` is about parity evidence, not whether
go-bpfman implements them. `Verdict` summarises who is ahead where they
differ: `parity` (equal), `Go ahead` (Go demonstrably correct, Rust
wrong), `unclear` (differ with no demonstrable right answer), `Go-only`
(Rust unsupported). No case was found where Rust is ahead of Go.

### Support

| Program type | Go lifecycle | Rust lifecycle | Kernel (bpftool) | Flag coverage | Gaps |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| xdp | pass | pass | match | covered | Rust rejects `SEC("xdp/<name>")` objects |
| tc | pass | pass | match | covered | none |
| tcx | pass | pass | match | covered | duplicate attach to one hook rejected (native mprog) |
| tracepoint | pass | pass | match | covered | none |
| kprobe | pass | pass | match | covered | none |
| kretprobe | pass | downgraded | differ: link[0].retprobe | partial | Rust loads `kretprobe:` as a plain kprobe |
| uprobe | pass | pass | match | covered | `--container-pid` is Go-only |
| uretprobe | pass | downgraded | differ: link[0].retprobe | partial | Rust loads `uretprobe:` as a plain uprobe |
| fentry | pass | pass | match | covered | none |
| fexit | pass | pass | match | covered | none |

"Lifecycle" is `load -> get program -> attach -> get link -> list ->
detach -> unload`, compared on normalised semantic state. The "Kernel
(bpftool)" column is computed, not asserted: `parity-compare` captures
each program's kernel + bpffs footprint with `bpftool` (program tag,
map shape, link semantics) for both implementations and diffs them, so
`match` means the kernel objects are identical and `differ` names the
exact field. "downgraded" means the command succeeds but Rust produces
an entry probe rather than a return probe.

### Flag coverage

| Area | Status |
| --- | --- |
| output format | Go `-o json`/`text`; Rust `get`/`list` are text-only |
| load and attach `--metadata` | parity |
| `--application` grouping and list filter | parity |
| `--global` | parity for bare hex; Rust rejects the `0x` prefix |
| `--map-owner-id` | Go shares the kernel map; Rust does not (Rust bug) |
| priority and ordering (xdp/tc/tcx) | parity (dispatcher and native mprog) |
| proceed-on (default and custom) | parity |
| TC/TCX direction (ingress/egress) | parity |
| `--netns` (xdp/tc/tcx) | parity |
| kprobe `--offset`, uprobe `--pid`, uprobe offset-only | parity |
| `--container-pid` | Go-only (Rust unsupported) |
| image load and `--pull-policy` | parity |
| `--registry-auth` | not yet exercised |

### Known differences

The differences, with reproductions and a note on what "divergence"
does and does not claim, are in
[`docs/parity/ISSUES.md`](docs/parity/ISSUES.md). In short:

- kretprobe / uretprobe: Rust accepts the `kretprobe:` / `uretprobe:` token but loads an entry probe, so no return-probe attachment is made.
- `--map-owner-id`: Rust records the owner but loads a fresh map, so the programs do not share a kernel map (Go does); this is a Rust bug.
- `--container-pid`: implemented in Go, unsupported in Rust.
- XDP section format: Rust requires bare `SEC("xdp")`; Go also accepts `SEC("xdp/<name>")`.
- Error exit codes differ for some failure classes, though both fail.

### Proof: xdp baseline

Shared object `e2e/testdata/bpf/xdp_pass.bpf.o`, program `xdp:pass`, attached to a throwaway veth (`sudo ip link add bpfmanpar0 type veth peer name bpfmanpar0p && sudo ip link set bpfmanpar0 up`). Excerpts below; full output in the linked transcripts.

Go:

```console
$ ./bin/go-bpfman program load file e2e/testdata/bpf/xdp_pass.bpf.o --programs xdp:pass -o json
...
"program_id": 260491,
"program_type": "xdp",
...
$ ./bin/go-bpfman link attach xdp <program-id> bpfmanpar0 --priority 100 -o json
...
"id": 2123456948,
"interface": "bpfmanpar0",
"priority": 100,
"position": 0,
...
```

Rust:

```console
$ ./bin/bpfman-rs load file --path e2e/testdata/bpf/xdp_pass.bpf.o --programs xdp:pass
...
Program ID: 260501
BPF Function: pass
Program Type: xdp
...
$ ./bin/bpfman-rs attach <program-id> xdp --iface bpfmanpar0 --priority 100
...
Link ID: 157373504
Interface: bpfmanpar0
Priority: 100
Position: 0
...
```

Result: pass. Full transcripts:
[`xdp-baseline.go.out`](docs/parity/outputs/xdp-baseline.go.out),
[`xdp-baseline.rust.out`](docs/parity/outputs/xdp-baseline.rust.out).

### Proof: tc baseline

Shared object `e2e/testdata/bpf/tc_counter.bpf.o`, program `tc:stats`, attached ingress to a throwaway veth. Excerpts below; full output in the linked transcripts.

Go:

```console
$ ./bin/go-bpfman program load file e2e/testdata/bpf/tc_counter.bpf.o --programs tc:stats -o json
...
"program_id": 260534,
"program_type": "tc",
...
$ ./bin/go-bpfman link attach tc <program-id> bpfmanpar0 ingress --priority 100 -o json
...
"id": 2123456949,
"direction": "ingress",
"priority": 100,
"position": 0,
...
```

Rust:

```console
$ ./bin/bpfman-rs load file --path e2e/testdata/bpf/tc_counter.bpf.o --programs tc:stats
...
Program ID: 260544
Program Type: tc
...
$ ./bin/bpfman-rs attach <program-id> tc --direction ingress --iface bpfmanpar0 --priority 100
...
Link ID: 769591044
Direction: ingress
Priority: 100
Position: 0
...
```

Result: pass. Full transcripts:
[`tc-baseline.go.out`](docs/parity/outputs/tc-baseline.go.out),
[`tc-baseline.rust.out`](docs/parity/outputs/tc-baseline.rust.out).

### Proof: kprobe baseline

Shared object `e2e/testdata/bpf/kprobe_counter.bpf.o`, program `kprobe:kprobe_counter`, attached to the kernel function `vfs_read`. Excerpts below; full output in the linked transcripts.

Go:

```console
$ ./bin/go-bpfman program load file e2e/testdata/bpf/kprobe_counter.bpf.o --programs kprobe:kprobe_counter -o json
...
"program_id": 260657,
"program_type": "kprobe",
...
$ ./bin/go-bpfman link attach kprobe <program-id> vfs_read -o json
...
"id": 2123456952,
"fn_name": "vfs_read",
"offset": 0,
"retprobe": false
...
```

Rust:

```console
$ ./bin/bpfman-rs load file --path e2e/testdata/bpf/kprobe_counter.bpf.o --programs kprobe:kprobe_counter
...
Program ID: 260667
Program Type: kprobe
...
$ ./bin/bpfman-rs attach <program-id> kprobe --fn-name vfs_read
...
Link ID: 411178162
Attach Function: vfs_read
Offset: 0
...
```

Result: pass. Full transcripts:
[`kprobe-baseline.go.out`](docs/parity/outputs/kprobe-baseline.go.out),
[`kprobe-baseline.rust.out`](docs/parity/outputs/kprobe-baseline.rust.out).

### Proof: kretprobe baseline

Shared object `e2e/testdata/bpf/kprobe_counter.bpf.o`, program `kretprobe:kprobe_counter`, attached to `vfs_read` -- the same object as the kprobe case, loaded as a return probe. Excerpts below; full output in the linked transcripts.

Go:

```console
$ ./bin/go-bpfman program load file e2e/testdata/bpf/kprobe_counter.bpf.o --programs kretprobe:kprobe_counter -o json
...
"program_id": 260698,
"program_type": "kretprobe",
...
$ ./bin/go-bpfman link attach kprobe <program-id> vfs_read -o json
...
"id": 2123456953,
"kind": "kretprobe",
"fn_name": "vfs_read",
"retprobe": true
...
```

Rust:

```console
$ ./bin/bpfman-rs load file --path e2e/testdata/bpf/kprobe_counter.bpf.o --programs kretprobe:kprobe_counter
...
Program ID: 260708
Program Type: kprobe
...
$ ./bin/bpfman-rs attach <program-id> kprobe --fn-name vfs_read
...
Link ID: 3266303794
Attach Function: vfs_read
Offset: 0
...
```

Result: Go yields a kretprobe (retprobe true); Rust loads it as a plain kprobe (ISSUE-2). Full transcripts:
[`kretprobe-baseline.go.out`](docs/parity/outputs/kretprobe-baseline.go.out),
[`kretprobe-baseline.rust.out`](docs/parity/outputs/kretprobe-baseline.rust.out).

### Proof: uprobe baseline

Shared object `e2e/testdata/bpf/uprobe_counter.bpf.o`, program `uprobe:uprobe_counter`, attached to `malloc` in the host C library. Excerpts below; full output in the linked transcripts.

Go:

```console
$ ./bin/go-bpfman program load file e2e/testdata/bpf/uprobe_counter.bpf.o --programs uprobe:uprobe_counter -o json
...
"program_id": 260739,
"program_type": "uprobe",
...
$ ./bin/go-bpfman link attach uprobe <program-id> <libc> --fn-name malloc -o json
...
"id": 2123456954,
"target": "/nix/store/l0l2ll1lmylczj1ihqn351af2kyp5x19-glibc-2.42-51/lib/libc.so.6",
"fn_name": "malloc",
"retprobe": false,
...
```

Rust:

```console
$ ./bin/bpfman-rs load file --path e2e/testdata/bpf/uprobe_counter.bpf.o --programs uprobe:uprobe_counter
...
Program ID: 260749
Program Type: uprobe
...
$ ./bin/bpfman-rs attach <program-id> uprobe --target <libc> --fn-name malloc
...
Link ID: 1066187451
Target: /nix/store/l0l2ll1lmylczj1ihqn351af2kyp5x19-glibc-2.42-51/lib/libc.so.6
Attach Function: malloc
Offset: 0
...
```

Result: pass. Full transcripts:
[`uprobe-baseline.go.out`](docs/parity/outputs/uprobe-baseline.go.out),
[`uprobe-baseline.rust.out`](docs/parity/outputs/uprobe-baseline.rust.out).

### Reproducing

Build the `bin/go-bpfman` and `bin/bpfman-rs` symlinks, then run
`bash docs/parity/run-all.sh`. Details, including the host assumptions
and the interface and namespace setup recipes, are in
[`docs/parity/README.md`](docs/parity/README.md) and
[`docs/parity/ENVIRONMENT.md`](docs/parity/ENVIRONMENT.md).
